scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained procedural and did not invent scientific findings beyond the provided study framing.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays in protocol or application drafting scope and does not provide patient-care advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflows preserve review-design or funding-application conventions instead of improvising unsupported methodology.

code_usability.detail: The primary deliverables are structured documents rather than scientific analysis code.

functional_suitability.note: The package covers core registration drafting steps and preserves placeholder semantics.

reliability.note: The date helper works, and the generation workflow is simple enough to stay stable.

performance_context.note: The date utility is the only script invocation needed, keeping execution overhead negligible for a single registration draft.

agent_usability.note: The workflow is concise and keeps placeholder and language rules visible during execution.

human_usability.note: The three-step workflow and explicit placeholder rules ({} vs <>) make the skill easy to follow without prior INPLASY experience.

security.note: The package does not expose secrets and keeps the task bounded to registry drafting.

maintainability.note: The implementation is small, but nomenclature drift weakens maintainability slightly.

agent_specific.note: Placeholder rules and language constraints are strong; branding consistency still needs cleanup.

key_strengths: The date utility gives the package one deterministic operational step instead of a fully prompt-only flow.
Placeholder-handling rules are explicit, which protects registry-form compatibility.
The skill keeps outputs within administrative drafting scope rather than pretending to validate methodology.

recommendations.title: Remove remaining PROSPERO naming leakage
recommendations.problem: The visible title and some phrasing still refer to PROSPERO despite the INPLASY-specific intent.
recommendations.root_cause: The package appears to be adapted from a related registration helper without a full terminology pass.
recommendations.fix: Rename visible headings and examples so the package is consistently INPLASY-specific throughout.
